message:
caam-g8mv: Disable Claude token refresh (CLAUDE-006)

Claude token refresh API is undocumented/speculative. refreshClaude()
now returns UnsupportedError immediately instead of calling the
speculative oauth endpoint. Users should use /login to re-authenticate.

- Added ClaudeRefreshDisabled sentinel in claude.go
- Updated refreshClaude() to return UnsupportedError
- Added tests verifying disabled behavior
- Updated CLAUDE_AUTH_INVENTORY.md with resolution status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-g2yz.3","title":"Profile Cloning","description":"## Status Update
Core cloning feature is IMPLEMENTED. Only remaining work: add passthrough symlink setup after clone.

Blocked by: file reservation conflict with BlackMountain on root.go.

---

## Overview
Clone an existing profile to create a new one with similar configuration.

## Implementation Status
✅ CloneOptions struct in profile.go
✅ Store.Clone method in profile.go
✅ profileCloneCmd in root.go
✅ Flags: --with-auth, --description, --force
⏳ Passthrough symlink setup (blocked)

## Remaining Work
Add call to passthrough.Manager.SetupPassthroughs(cloned.HomePath()) in profileCloneCmd after successful clone.

## Testing
All profile tests pass. Need to add specific clone tests.","notes":"Added passthrough symlink setup after clone.  {"id":"caam-g4sl","title":"Unit tests for internal/prediction engine","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for internal/prediction Engine

## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for the depletion prediction engine, burn rate calculation, and alert generation.

## Test Categories

### 1. Burn Rate Calculation Tests
```go
func TestBurnRateCalculation(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name           string
        entries        []*logs.LogEntry
        window         time.Duration
        wantTokens     float64
        wantConfidence float64
    }{
        {
            name: "steady burn rate",
            entries: generateSteadyUsage(1000, 10, time.Hour), // 1000 tokens each, 10 entries over 1 hour
            window: time.Hour,
            wantTokens: 10000.0 / 60, // ~167 tokens/min
            wantConfidence: 0.9,       // High confidence (steady)
        },
        {
            name: "variable burn rate",
            entries: generateVariableUsage(...),
            wantConfidence: 0.5, // Lower confidence (variable)
        },
        {
            name: "no recent data",
            entries: nil,
            wantConfidence: 0.0,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] Input: %d entries over %v", len(tc.entries), tc.window)
            
            rate := prediction.CalculateBurnRate(tc.entries, tc.window)
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Result: %.2f tokens/min, confidence=%.2f", 
                rate.TokensPerMinute, rate.Confidence)
            
            assertApprox(t, tc.wantTokens, rate.TokensPerMinute, 0.1)
            assertApprox(t, tc.wantConfidence, rate.Confidence, 0.1)
        })
    }
}
```

### 2. Depletion Prediction Tests
- Predict depletion with known burn rate
- Predict with zero burn rate (infinite time)
- Predict when already at 100%
- Predict with window reset before depletion

### 3. Warning Level Tests
```go
func TestWarningLevels(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        timeUntil   time.Duration
        wantWarning WarningLevel
    }{
        {2 * time.Hour, WarningNone},
        {25 * time.Minute, WarningApproaching},
        {5 * time.Minute, WarningImminent},
        {0, WarningImminent},
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.timeUntil.String(), func(t *testing.T) {
            pred := &Prediction{
                PredictedTime: time.Now().Add(tc.timeUntil),
            }
            t.Logf("[TEST] Time until depletion: %v", tc.timeUntil)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Expected warning: %v", tc.wantWarning)
            
            warning := pred.GetWarningLevel()
            t.Logf("[TEST] Actual warning: %v", warning)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tc.wantWarning, warning)
        })
    }
}
```

### 4. Alert Generation Tests
- Generate alert when approaching limit
- No duplicate alerts for same condition
- Clear alert when condition resolves
- Multiple profiles with different alerts

### 5. Confidence Calculation Tests
- High sample size = high confidence
- Low variance = high confidence
- Recent data = higher weight
- Stale data = lower confidence

## Logging Requirements
- Log all input parameters
- Log intermediate calculations
- Log final predictions with confidence intervals
- Log warning level determinations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Burn rate calculation tested with various patterns
- [ ] Depletion prediction accuracy verified
- [ ] Warning levels trigger at correct thresholds
- [ ] Alert generation logic tested
- [ ] Confidence scoring tested
- [ ] Edge cases handled (no data, stale data)

## Files to Create
- internal/prediction/engine_test.go
- internal/prediction/burnrate_test.go
- internal/prediction/alerts_test.go
- internal/prediction/testdata/usage_patterns.go

-{"id":"caam-g8mv","title":"Task: Reassess Claude token expiry/refresh features","description":"Goal
- Validate whether Claude token expiry parsing and refresh are accurate; disable if not.

Details
- Audit health.ParseClaudeExpiry + refresh.RefreshClaudeToken assumptions against current .credentials.json fields.
- If expiry/refresh cannot be verified, hard-disable refresh for Claude with a clear message.
- Ensure health scoring and status output do not imply valid expiry when data is opaque.
- Update docs/CLI to avoid implying proactive refresh works for Claude if it does not.
- Emit structured logs when refresh is skipped/disabled (reason, provider, file paths).

Tests
- Unit tests for expiry parsing with modern Claude credentials (opaque tokens).
- Unit tests for refresh-disabled path (explicit warning + no file writes).
- E2E test that simulates Claude auth files and ensures status output is honest.

Success
- Refresh logic is either correct and tested, or explicitly disabled with user-facing rationale.

 {"id":"caam-g9dl","title":"Task: caam setup distributed (auto-config)","description":"Goal
- Provide a single command that configures coordinator(s) and local agent across machines.

Details
- Deploy binary to remotes via SSH/SFTP if needed with version checks and checksum verification.
- Generate config files for coordinator + agent.
- Install/enable systemd user units on Linux and optional launchd on macOS.
- Support dry-run and explicit remote list overrides.
- Require explicit confirmation before any remote changes unless --yes is provided.
- Surface missing dependency checks (wezterm CLI, tailscale, systemd) before attempting changes.

Success
- One command brings the distributed auth system online end-to-end with safe defaults.

 {"id":"caam-gir","title":"Runtime Enhancements","description":"# Runtime Enhancements

## Purpose
Improve runtime behavior with hot reload, file watching, and signal handling for a smoother user experience.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool supports hot reload without restart:
```go
POST /admin/reload → h.pool.replace(newAccounts)
```

## Why This Matters
Users often:
1. Run TUI in one terminal
2. Add/modify profiles in another terminal
3. Expect TUI to reflect changes

Without hot reload, users must restart TUI to see changes.

## What This Epic Delivers
1. File watching for profiles directory (fsnotify)
2. Automatic TUI refresh on changes
3. `caam profiles reload` CLI command
4. SIGHUP handling for daemon mode
5. Graceful reload (no state loss)

## Technical Approach

### File Watching
Use fsnotify to watch:
- `~/.caam/profiles/` directory
- Provider-specific auth directories (optional)

Events to handle:
- CREATE: New profile added
- MODIFY: Profile updated
- DELETE: Profile removed
- RENAME: Profile renamed

### TUI Integration
```go
func (m *Model) watchProfiles() {
    watcher, _ := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    watcher.Add(profilesDir)
    
    for event := range watcher.Events {
        m.reloadProfiles()  // async refresh
    }
}
```

### Signal Handling
```go
sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
signal.Notify(sigChan, syscall.SIGHUP)

go func() {
    for range sigChan {
        reloadConfiguration()
    }
}()
```

## User Experience
```
# Terminal 1: TUI running
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────┐
│ ● work@company.com   🟢   │
│   personal@gmail.com 🟡   │
└───────────────────────────┘

# Terminal 2: Add new profile
$ caam backup claude newproject@corp.com
Profile saved.

# Terminal 1: Auto-updates!
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────┐
│ ● work@company.com   🟢   │
│   personal@gmail.com 🟡   │
│   newproject@corp.com 🟢  │  ← NEW
└───────────────────────────┘
```

## Edge Cases
- Rapid file changes: Debounce (100ms)
- Corrupted profile: Log warning, skip
- Permission denied: Log error, continue watching
- Watch limit exceeded: Fall back to polling

## Dependencies
- None (independent enhancement)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:16:38.029349036Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:45:49.816073234Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:45:49.816073234Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented: watcher package (fsnotify-based with debounce), TUI hot reload integration (automatic refresh on profile changes), profile add/modify/delete detection. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-gir","depends_on_id":"caam-3am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:26:53.664294779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-tg77.3","title":"Task: Onboarding wizard (gum/huh) + non-interactive mode","description":"Goal
- Guide users through setup with a polished, low-friction wizard.

Details
- Add `caam setup` interactive flow using gum (shell) or Huh (TUI) to:
  - detect agents
  - choose which providers to manage
  - configure watch/backup defaults
  - optionally enable distributed recovery
  - choose isolation strategy (HOME/XDG vs CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) and shared_items policy
- Provide a non-interactive mode (`--yes` + flags) that prints actions.
- Save the resulting config and show next-step commands.
- Output a reproducible "plan" summary for later reruns.

Tests
- Simulated interactive flows (expect scripts) and non-interactive snapshot tests.
- Validate that repeated runs are idempotent.
- Structured logs for each step so automation can diagnose failures.

Success
- New users can get fully configured in under 2 minutes.

 {"id":"caam-tg77.4","title":"Task: Auth watcher reliability + cross-platform fallbacks","description":"Goal
- Make auth file watching reliable across Linux/macOS and tolerant of atomic writes.

Details
- Add debounce + coalescing for rapid file changes.
- Handle atomic rename/write patterns (common in OAuth clients) without missing events.
- Provide polling fallback when fsnotify is unreliable or unsupported.
- Add per-provider file sets and ignore lists; skip noisy optional files unless configured.
- Emit structured watcher logs with provider, event type, file set, and matched profile.

Tests
- Simulate rename+write sequences and ensure watcher still triggers.
- Include a polling fallback test path.
- Add a high-frequency churn test to validate debounce behavior and log output.

Success
- Watcher detects logins consistently without spurious backups.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:53:33.160027535Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:54:07.880598178Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-tg77.4","depends_on_id":"caam-tg77","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:53:33.180411443Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-tg77.5","title":"Task: Gum-enhanced CLI flows + fallback UX","description":"Goal
- Make non-TUI CLI interactions feel premium while remaining scriptable.

Details
- Use gum for optional interactive prompts (confirmations, choices, input) in commands like setup, pick, backup, and update.
- Detect TTY and NO_COLOR; fall back to plain text prompts automatically.
- Provide a `--no-gum` / `--plain` mode for automation.
- Ensure outputs are stable for scripts when `--json` or `--plain` is set.
- Emit structured logs for each prompt step (action, choice, result) in debug mode.

Tests
- Simulate gum present/absent and CI environments to verify fallback behavior.
- Snapshot tests for prompt text in plain mode.
- E2E test for scripted automation path (no prompts).

Success
- CLI flows feel polished without breaking automation.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:56:43.651804927Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:54:13.670085952Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-tg77.5","depends_on_id":"caam-tg77","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:56:43.653147215Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"caam-tg77.6","title":"Task: Claude config-dir isolation mode (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) + fallback","description":"Goal
- Support a robust, industrial-grade CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR isolation mode with safe fallbacks.

Background
- A community gist suggests per-account config directories via CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR and symlinked shared items.
- Official docs emphasize ~/.claude and .claude project dirs; CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is not clearly documented and has open bugs.

Details
- Implement a Go-based "config-dir manager" that:
  - lays out per-profile config dirs consistently
  - validates dir health (permissions, required files, no unexpected cross-links)
  - selects isolation strategy: env (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) vs HOME/XDG
- Add config flag: claude.config_dir_mode = auto|env|home (default auto).
- In auto mode: set CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR if supported/healthy; otherwise use HOME + XDG_CONFIG_HOME isolation.
- Add a compatibility probe (version + behavior check) and warn if CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR still creates local .claude dirs or breaks /ide.
- Ensure isolation logic is consistent across caam exec, setup, and run wrappers.
- Emit structured logs that indicate which isolation strategy was selected and why.

Tests
- Unit tests for env selection logic and config parsing.
- File-system tests for config-dir manager health checks.
- E2E test using a mocked claude binary that prints env vars to ensure correct propagation.
- Regression test for fallback when CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is flagged unhealthy.

Success
- Users can adopt the CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR strategy when it works, without breaking workflows when it does not.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:34:28.111743337Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T01:37:31.832357828Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-tg77.6","depends_on_id":"caam-tg77","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:34:28.113103509Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"caam-tg77.6","title":"Task: Claude config-dir isolation mode (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) + fallback","description":"Goal
- Support a robust, industrial-grade CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR isolation mode with safe fallbacks.

Background
- A community gist suggests per-account config directories via CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR and symlinked shared items.
- Official docs emphasize ~/.claude and .claude project dirs; CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is not clearly documented and has open bugs.

Details
- Implement a Go-based "config-dir manager" that:
  - lays out per-profile config dirs consistently
  - validates dir health (permissions, required files, no unexpected cross-links)
  - selects isolation strategy: env (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) vs HOME/XDG
- Add config flag: claude.config_dir_mode = auto|env|home (default auto).
- In auto mode: set CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR if supported/healthy; otherwise use HOME + XDG_CONFIG_HOME isolation.
- Add a compatibility probe (version + behavior check) and warn if CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR still creates local .claude dirs or breaks /ide.
- Ensure isolation logic is consistent across caam exec, setup, and run wrappers.
- Emit structured logs that indicate which isolation strategy was selected and why.

Tests
- Unit tests for env selection logic and config parsing.
- File-system tests for config-dir manager health checks.
- E2E test using a mocked claude binary that prints env vars to ensure correct propagation.
- Regression test for fallback when CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is flagged unhealthy.

Success
- Users can adopt the CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR strategy when it works, without breaking workflows when it does not.

 {"id":"caam-tg77.7","title":"Task: Claude shared-items policy (allowlist + symlinks)","description":"Goal
- Let users explicitly share safe Claude assets (agents, commands, styles) across isolated profiles without sharing history/projects.

Background
- Community gist suggests symlinking selected directories via a shared_items allowlist.
- Sharing history/projects between accounts can cause confusing cross-account state.

Details
- Implement a Go "shared-items manager" with:
  - allowlist + hard denylist (projects/history/sessions/logs never shared)
  - atomic symlink creation and rollback on failure
  - conflict handling: if a real dir exists, back it up before linking
  - optional copy fallback when symlinks are not permitted
- Add config: claude.shared_items = ["agents", "commands", "styles", "plugins", "skills"] (default empty).
- Provide CLI helpers: `caam claude share --list/--add/--remove` with dry‑run.
- Emit structured logs for each symlink created, skipped, or rejected by policy.

Tests
- Unit tests for allowlist/denylist validation.
- Filesystem tests that verify symlink creation, backup, rollback, and copy fallback.
- E2E test: repeated runs are idempotent and preserve existing files.

Success
- Users can safely share reusable assets without leaking account history or state.

 {"id":"caam-tg77.8","title":"Task: Claude login file-diff tracer (discover all touched paths)","description":"Goal
- Provide a safe tool to discover exactly which Claude files change during login/logout.

Details
- Add `caam claude trace-login` (or `caam trace claude`) that:
  - snapshots candidate dirs (~/.claude, ~/.config/claude-code, project .claude)
  - pauses for user to perform login/logout
  - produces a diff report (added/changed/removed files + sizes + hashes)
- Support `--exclude` patterns and a `--no-projects` flag to avoid large repo scans.
- Output a JSON report for tooling + a human summary table.
- Emit a derived "watch rules" section listing the minimal file set for caam watch.
- Never deletes files; uses read-only snapshots.

Tests
- Unit tests for diff computation on synthetic directories.
- E2E test using a temp dir to simulate changes and verifying report output + watch rules.
- Log output tests to ensure sensitive content is never printed.

Success
- Users can empirically confirm which files need isolation or backup.

 {"id":"caam-tg77.9","title":"Task: Docs + guardrails for Claude multi-instance isolation","description":"Goal
- Prevent confusing cross-account state by documenting isolation rules and enforcing safe defaults.

Details
- Document which Claude directories are account‑sensitive (history, projects, sessions, logs).
- Recommend isolation defaults (separate config dirs) and safe shared_items allowlist.
- Add CLI warnings when shared_items includes risky paths.
- Provide a quick-start snippet for multi-instance usage (caam exec/caam profile shell).

Tests
- Doc lint check ensures the warning section is present.
- Unit tests for validation of shared_items policy (reject forbidden entries).

Success
- Users can run multiple instances without corrupting or cross‑contaminating state.
